title: Migrate compile-time System.get_env to runtime.exs
prompt: `test_fixtures/secrets_compile_time_env.ex` has THREE issues: (1) `System.get_env("STRIPE_KEY")` is called in `config.exs`, which means it is read at COMPILE time — the value is baked into the release at build time, so runtime env changes are ignored, (2) `System.get_env("DATABASE_URL")` has the same problem, (3) the webhook secret has a `||` fallback to a hardcoded dev value. Migrate all three config values from `config.exs` to `runtime.exs`. In `runtime.exs`, use `System.fetch_env!/1` so missing env raises rather than silently returning `nil`. Write the result to `config/runtime.exs`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires understanding compile-time vs runtime evaluation AND migrating correctly.
research_citation: Elixir Forum: 'Configuration files... are evaluated at compile-time. Thus, if you use System.get_env within a configuration file, it is evaluated during the compilation process.'
